Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 340 [Blessings for the Assyrian king][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

1 x [...] ⸢LUGAL⸣ EN 1 gišGAG.TI-ka KUR na-ki-⸢ri⸣ li-šam-qit ina rig₂-me ša gišPAN-ka liš-tar-i-bu KUR-MEŠ nak-ru-tu1

1 ... [...] the king, the lord! May 1 arrow from you cause the downfall of the enemy's land; may the hostile mountains be made to shake at the sound of your bow.

o 22

2 ⸢KI.MIN⸣<(...)> [2 diš₈-tar₂-MEŠ]-ka liš-⸢ta⸣-a ina zar-ba-bi <KAŠ?> du-uš-šu-pa gišGEŠTIN KU₃

2 ditto. May your [2 goddesses] drink sweetened beer (and) pure wine from zarbābu-vessels.

o 33

⸢3⸣ KI.MIN<(...)> U₄? [3-KAM] ina IGI A-MEŠ ŠUBdi I₃.GIŠ lu₂ḪAL⸢ut⸣ SILIMme u₃ EŠ.BAR li-pu-ul-ka dša₂-maš

3 ditto. On the [3rd] day, you will throw oil on the surface of the water. May Šamaš answer you with a bārûtu-divination of well-being and decision.

o 44

4 KI.MIN<(...)> 4 lu-u LA₂ta ina IGI EREN₂-ḪIni UŠ.UŠ⸢di⸣ ina KUR ša tu-ṣar-ra-mu ŠUBdi gišAŠ.TE-ka

4 ditto. May 4 (horses) be harnessed (so that) you may constantly drive in front of the army. You will set up your throne in the land that you strive for.

o 55

5 KI.MIN<(...)> 5 ⸢ŠEŠ-MEŠ⸣-ka!(E) li-kil-lu <reš>-ka li-nar-ru a-a-⸢bi⸣-ka li-šam-qit-tu₂ ⸢ge⸣-re-e-ka

5 ditto. May your 5 brothers look after you, kill your enemies (and) cause the downfall of your opponents.

o 66

⸢6⸣ KI.MIN<(...)> NAM⸢at⸣ la-ba-ri DINGIR.MAḪ li-šim ši-mat-⸢ka⸣ dba-ba₆ u₃ dNIN.GIR₂.SU liš-pu-ku!(UK) ŠE!(MU)u-ka

6 ditto. May Belet-ili determine (as) your fate the fate of becoming old. May Baba and Ningirsu pile up your barley.

o 77

[7] ⸢KI.MIN⸣<(...)> 7 LUGAL-MEŠni lid-gul pa-nu-uk-ka ki-⸢i⸣ NAM.RI liš-šu-nik-ka bil-tu₄

7 ditto. May 7 kings obey you (and) bring you tribute like booty.

o 88

8 ⸢KI.MIN⸣<(...)> ⸢sa⸣-lim ⸢du⸣-<un>-qu dUTU li-šar-ši-ka-ma UN-MEŠ ṣal-mat SAG.DU re-ʾe-⸢e⸣ GIM U₈.UDU-ḪI.A

8 ditto. May Šamaš provide you with peace and goodness; tend the black-headed people like a flock.

o 99

9 KI.MIN<(...)> U₄ 9-⸢KAM₂ ša₂⸣ dbe-lat—TI.LA u₄-me-ka a-na dum-⸢qi₂⸣ MU-MEŠ-ka a-na ku-šir₄ li-im-nu

9 ditto. The 9th day is the one of Belat-balaṭi.1 May she turn your days into goodness, your years into success.

o 1010

10 KI.MIN<(...)> eš-rat ⸢ku⸣-ru-ur GUR₇-MEŠ-ka ⸢šeKURUM₆ lu? šad⸣-lu₄ liš-ša₂-kin₂ ḪE₂.GAL₂

10 ditto. Pile up the tithes! May your grain-heaps be abundant with barley rations; may abundance be established.

o 1111

11 KI.MIN<(...)> ki-i 1en UN-MEŠ ⸢nap⸣-ḫar KUR.KUR-MEŠ ana qi₂-bit-ka a-na IGI lu₂A KIN-ka ap-pa lil-bi-nu

11 ditto. As one, may the people of all the lands stroke (their) nose at your command before your messenger.

o 1212

12 KI.MIN<(...)> 12 DANNA MUL-MEŠ ⸢ziq⸣-pi ša₂ KASKAL šu-ut dEN.LIL₂ u₄-me-šam MUNUS.SIG₅-MEŠ-⸢ka i⸣-[na IGI] ⸢EN⸣ EN-MEŠ liq-bu-u DU-MEŠ A₂-MIN-ka

12 ditto. 12 are the double-hours of the culminating stars of the path of Ellil. May they daily decree your good fortune before the lord of lords (and) may they go at your side.

o 1313

⸢13⸣ KI.MIN<(...)> <U₄> 13-KAM ša d30 MEN ⸢IL₂? a?-na?⸣ LUGAL AN⸢e lip-qi₂⸣-du SILIM-ka

13 ditto. The 13th day is the one of Sin bearing a corona. May they entrust your well-being to the king of heaven.

o 1414

[14] ⸢KI.MIN⸣<(...)> ša₂-⸢ka⸣-an ta-mit DUMU ⸢lu₂⸣[ḪAL]-⸢MEŠ?⸣ ina ⸢IGI⸣ dUTU u d⸢IŠKUR an-na?⸣ [...] ⸢li-pu-ul⸣-ka-ma ⸢ku⸣-<<x>>-šu-ud er-nit-tuk

[14] ditto. Performing an oracular query. May a member of the (group of) diviners answer you with a [...] yes before Šamaš and Adad. Achieve your victory!

o 1515

[15 KI.MIN<(...)> ...] x lib₃-bi-ša₂ x x [...] x lib₃-ba-⸢ka⸣-ma ka-bit-ta-ka lip-⸢šaḫ⸣

[15 ditto.] ... of her heart ... [...] ... your heart and may your mind relax.

o 1616

16 KI.MIN<(...)> nu-⸢bat⸣-[tu₂] ⸢ša dAMAR⸣.UTU x [...] ⸢d⸣30 ⸢SU.LIM IL₂ kul⸣-lat a-a-bi-ka i-na ŠU-ka li-⸢im⸣-nu

16 ditto. The eve of Marduk's festival ... [...]. May Sin, who is bearing radiance, surrender all your enemies to you.

o 1717

[17] ⸢KI.MIN⸣<(...)> ⸢DU₈.DU₈ir?⸣ U₄.⸢EŠ₃⸣.[EŠ₃] ša!(TUM) dMUATI EN E₂.⸢ZI⸣.DA ⸢rag-ga⸣ u ṣe-na i-na-as-sa-ḫu la ma-⸢gi⸣-ri-ka ana GIR₃-MIN-ka ⸢li⸣-kan-niš

[17] ditto. May he absolve (your sins). Eššēšu-festival of Nabu, lord of Ezida, (who) expels villain and evildoer. May he subject the unsubmissive people to your feet.

o 1818

⸢18⸣ KI.MIN<(...)> EZEN ḫu-un-tu₂ ša dUTU ⸢DI⸣.KU₅.GAL ⸢DINGIR⸣-MEŠ ana ⸢gi-mir⸣ [EREN₂]-ḪIni-ka liš-ku-na ša₂-lim-⸢ta⸣

18 ditto. humṭu-festival of Šamaš, the chief judge of the gods. May he decree well-being for all of your army.

o 1919

19 KI.MIN<(...)> u₄-mu ug-gat ša dbe-⸢lat⸣—TI.LA it-ti da-nu₃ ⸢AD⸣-ša₂ li-sal-li-ma DINGIR ba-ni-ka

19 ditto. Day of Belat-balaṭi's rage. May she reconcile the god who created you with Anu, her father.

o 2020

20 KI.MIN<(...)> ki-i ša₂ U₄ 20-KAM₂ ⸢nam⸣-ri ana kiš-šat UN-MEŠ ⸢lit⸣-tan-bi-ṭu zi-mu-u-ka

20 ditto. May your appearance be bright to all the people just as the bright 20th day.2

o 2121

21 KI.MIN<(...)> u₄-mu ḪE₂.GAL₂ u₄-mu ez-zu ⸢u₄⸣-mu nam-ru ni-šu mur-ṣu ta-ne₂-ḫu <ina> NI₂-ZUka li-is-su-ḫu DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

21 ditto. Day of plenty,3 violent day, bright day.4 May the great gods expel a broken oath, illness (and) lament from your self.

o 2222

22 KI.MIN<(...)> u₄-mu un-ni-ni di-par₂ ⸢SILIM⸣me be-lat ŠE.GA u ŠE.⸢GA⸣ ša₂-lum-mat nam-ri-ri-ša₂ li-ḫal-lip-ka!(UR)

22 ditto. Day of supplication. May the torch of well-being, the lady of obedience and agreement, clad you in the light of her awe-inspiring radiance.

o 2323

23 KI.MIN<(...)> u₄-mu U₄ EZEN a-ki-te ša dMAŠ!(MEŠ) zik-ri qar-di na-ram [...] ⸢li⸣-dan-nin USU-ka li-nar-ru a-a-bi-ka li-šam-qit ⸢gar?⸣-re-ka

23 ditto. Day of the akītu-festival of Ninurta, heroic man, loved one of [...]. May he strengthen your strength; may he (lit. they) kill your enemies; may he cause your opponent's downfall.

o 2424

24 KI.MIN<(...)> i-si-⸢ni šar⸣-rat ⸢NIBRUki⸣ ina ma-ḫar dBAD [...]-⸢ka⸣ liq-ba-a du-muq-ka

24 ditto. Festival of Nippur's queen. [...] for you in front of Ellil (and) may she decree the best for you.

Reverse
r 1r 1

25 KI.MIN<(...)> u₄-mu ⸢i?⸣-[si?-ni? dbe-lat]—TI.⸢LA⸣ ḫi-rat!(NI) dPA.BIL₂.⸢SAG⸣ SUMUN bal-ṭi u MU.MU-MEŠ MU NUMUN NUNUZ!(BAL)e u ⸢na⸣-an-na-⸢ba⸣ lu-šar-šu-ka

25 ditto. Day of the festival of Belat-balaṭi, wife of Pabilsag. May they provide you with a long-lasting life and years, name, descendants, offspring, and progeny.

r 22

26 KI.MIN<(...)> ⸢U₄?⸣ x x x x u ḪE₂.⸢GAL₂⸣ li-qiš-ka EN DINGIR-MEŠ DI.KU₅ ANe u KIti₃ ina di-nim u EŠ.BAR ma-ḫar-ka li-ziz

26 ditto. Day of .... May ... give you ... and plenty. May the lord of the gods, judge of heaven and earth stand before you in lawsuit and decision.

r 33

⸢27⸣ KI.MIN<(...)> ⸢u₄⸣-[mu ...] x DINGIR-⸢MEŠ?⸣ [GAL]-⸢MEŠ⸣ ina UKKIN-šu₂-nu ši-mat la-ba-ri SUMUN : xliš-ra-ku-ka

27 ditto. Day [...] .... May the [great] gods in their assembly bestow upon you the fate of growing old (gloss: growing old : ...).

r 44

⸢28⸣ [KI.MIN<(...)> ... d]⸢INANA? be-lat? MURU₂ ME₃⸣ gišTUKUL-MEŠ KUR₂-MEŠ-ka li-šab-bir-⸢ma DAB?⸣-MEŠ UB-MEŠ

28 ditto. [...] Ištar, lady of battle and fight. May she smash the weapons of your enemies (so that) they occupy the corners.

r 55

⸢29⸣ KI.MIN<(...)> [...] ⸢de₂⸣-[a] x x [...] x [...] ša ABZU u₄-mu-šam-ma liq-⸢tab⸣-ba du-muq-ka

29 ditto. [...] Ea ... [...] ... [...] of the Apsu. May he always decree the best for you every day.

r 66

⸢30⸣ KI.MIN<(...)> U₄.⸢NA₂-AM₃ U₄ ta!-šil!-ti! DINGIR⸣[ti-ka dAŠ].⸢IM₂.BABBAR⸣ [li]-rik₂ U₄-MEŠ-ka lid-[diš ...]-⸢ka⸣ DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ ina UKKIN-šu₂-nu ši-mat BAD₃ u₄-me liš-ra-ku-ka

30 ditto. Day of the moon's disappearance, day of the celebration of [your] divinity. May Namraṣit lengthen your days and renew your [...]; may the great gods in their assembly bestow upon you the fate for all time.

(colophon)
r 77

ša₃-ṭir₂ mdNA₃—⸢KAR?⸣ir šam-lu₃-⸢u₂ BAN₃⸣.DA PEŠ mu-⸢um⸣-mu

Writer: Nabu-eṭir, the joung scribal apprentice, member of the mummu-institution

r 88

mx-[...] x [...] x E [...]-šu₂-nu

of ..., their ....

1Watanabe (1991:352-353) proposed to restore the beginning as D[I-mu a-na LUG]AL "Dem Herrn [Köni]g [möge es] wo[hl ergehen!]", with the caveat "Die vorgeschlagene Ergänzung ... beansprucht aber keine Gewißheit."

1 Here Gula (cf . Watanabe 1991 : 356).

2 The 20th day is the day of Šamaš the sun god (= ᵈ20).

3 Euphemism for ūmu lemnu "bad day " (see Watanabe 1991 : 359).

4 Euphemism for ūmu eklu/eṭû "dark day " (see Watanabe 1991 : 359).

Created by Greta Van Buylaere for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2010 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338655
